lyrics:
I lit my name on a wall of stone
Watched it dance in the firelight’s moan
Told myself I’d found the spark divine
But I was just afraid of time

You’ve said it so long, you believe that it’s true
An endless obsession - the myth becomes you
A factual fiction, the truth now denies
Your stories are fables… Prehistoric Lies

Now I preach through a wire and glass
Selling faith by the megabyte mass
Every word I speak got a barcode price
Every god I build demands a sacrifice

You’ve said it so long, you believe that it’s true
An endless obsession - the myth becomes you
A factual fiction, the truth now denies
Your stories are fables… Prehistoric Lies

Now I’m sifting through bones of belief
Dust and doubt all tangled beneath
Found my heart still beating in disguise
Wrapped in skin of prehistoric lies

You’ve said it so long, you believe that it’s true
An endless obsession - the myth becomes you
A factual fiction, the truth now denies
Your stories are fables… Prehistoric Lies

I dig through the noise for a human tone
But every echo sounds like my own
Maybe the truth just fossilized
Maybe I’m the lie I idolize

You’ve said it so long, you believe that it’s true
An endless obsession - the myth becomes you
A factual fiction, the truth now denies
We’re born, we burn, we breathe… Prehistoric Lies
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This soulful blues-rock track establishes a contemplative atmosphere with its clean, articulate electric guitar and expressive male vocals. The song maintains a relaxed, head-nodding groove, building in energy from the verse to a more passionate chorus and instrumental sections. The production feels spacious and organic, presenting a well-balanced mix where the foundational bass and drums create a solid pocket for the guitar and vocals to sit comfortably on top. A virtuosic and fiery guitar solo, full of rapid phrases and emotive bends, serves as the defining emotional peak of the song.

review.review_text: Running six minutes in E minor at a clip that feels more urgent than the blues-rock tag suggests, 'Prehistoric Lies' earns its runtime through a lyric that keeps tightening its own noose: 'a factual fiction, the truth now denies' lands harder each time the chorus comes around, because PCBender has actually built toward it. The verses do real work, moving from cave walls to megabyte faith with a coherence that a lot of concept-driven rock fumbles, and the guitar solo that arrives late in the track, all bent phrases and earned heat, lands the way a good solo should: like a statement, not a decoration. This is PCBender at a high watermark, the kind of song that reminds you why the six-minute album cut still matters.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'a factual fiction, the truth now denies'
quoted lyric: chorus progression from cave-wall imagery to megabyte faith
